items.title: USDA posts updated list of state SNAP food-restriction waivers (some starting as soon as July 1, 2026)
items.summary: USDA updated its tracker of SNAP food-restriction waivers that limit what items can be purchased with benefits (e.g., soda and candy). The tracker lists target implementation dates including Arkansas (07/01/26), Tennessee (07/31/26), and Colorado (10/30/26), so SNAP households and retailers in those states should watch for state-issued guidance on what products will be restricted.

items.title: CMS previews stricter budget-neutrality rules for Medicaid Section 1115 waivers starting Jan. 1, 2027
items.summary: CMS says that beginning January 1, 2027 it will not approve new Medicaid Section 1115 demonstrations, renewals, or amendments unless the CMS Chief Actuary certifies the project is budget neutral. CMS notes that nearly one-third of all federal Medicaid dollars flow through demonstration projects, and it plans to apply this approach to approvals until a final rule is effective.

items.title: IRS: April 15, 2026 tax deadline; EITC/ACTC refunds generally arrive by March 2 with direct deposit
items.summary: The IRS says it expects about 164 million individual tax returns for tax year 2025 ahead of the April 15, 2026 federal deadline. For early filers claiming the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) or Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) with direct deposit, the IRS says most refunds should be available by March 2, 2026, and “Where’s My Refund?” should show projected deposit dates for most by Feb. 21, 2026.

items.title: SSA urges remaining beneficiaries to switch from paper checks to electronic payments (Direct Deposit or Direct Express)
items.summary: SSA says federal law and Executive Order 14247 require federal benefits to be paid electronically as of Sept. 30, 2025, and Social Security plans to complete the full transition for all beneficiaries in 2026. SSA cites Treasury data saying paper checks are 16 times more likely to be lost, stolen, altered, or returned undeliverable, and says the average cost to print a check is $3.07.

items.title: SSA publishes the 2026–2027 Social Security benefit payment schedule
items.summary: SSA posted its official payment calendar for 2026–2027, including the Supplemental Security Income (SSI) payment dates and Social Security payment Wednesdays based on birth date. The schedule is useful for planning around months where payments shift due to weekends or federal holidays.

items.title: HUD updates guidance on the Asset Repositioning Fee (ARF) that can add operating subsidy during public-housing demolition/disposition
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-16 (issued May 29, 2026) updates guidance on eligibility and timing for the Asset Repositioning Fee (ARF), which can provide an add-on operating subsidy when units are removed via Section 18 demolition/disposition. The notice explains that ARF begins on the first day of the next quarter after 6 months from the trigger event, and that ARF lasts 36 months for demolitions and 24 months for dispositions.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: USDA final rule updates WIC regulations (reporting, confidentiality, and breastfeeding certification flexibility)
items.summary: USDA finalized a rule updating multiple WIC regulatory provisions, including streamlined state reporting requirements and clarified confidentiality rules to strengthen coordination with other organizations and physicians. The rule also incorporates legislation giving state agencies the option to extend the certification period for breastfeeding women.

items.title: FAFSA (2026–27 school year): You can submit as early as Oct. 1, 2025; federal deadline is June 30, 2027
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Education’s 2026–27 FAFSA form states you can submit as early as Oct. 1, 2025, and the federal deadline is June 30, 2027 (states and colleges may set earlier deadlines). Submitting earlier can help students access limited first-come, first-served aid like some state grants.
items.source: U.S. Department of Education — Federal Student Aid
